| | No parent should fear sending their child to school, and no child should feel unsafe in a classroom. Yet, Monday’s tragic school shooting in Wisconsin reminds us of the urgent need to better protect the spaces where our children are supposed to be learning, not living in fear. My heart breaks for the families of the victims, and the students who will carry this with them for the rest of their lives.
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98981074/518459783/2033897161?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzczOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWQyNDU5OC01YWJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4rAwEI_fltasdIKh1c6uIaFoA5j6MIb3LORfz_Z9q8k=&emci=ef15b4ed-50be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=e9d24598-5abe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1486204)

The safety of our children must be the top priority. As the threat of gun violence in schools continues, so must our actions to prevent school shootings in Congress. Join me in calling for more straightforward, common-sense gun safety laws that will help prevent these horrific events! Measures such as universal background checks, safe storage requirements, and red flag laws can save lives without infringing on rights. **[**Add your name to tell MAGA it must prioritize safe schools over the gun lobby!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98981075/518459784/-1227991233?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzczOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWQyNDU5OC01YWJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4rAwEI_fltasdIKh1c6uIaFoA5j6MIb3LORfz_Z9q8k=&emci=ef15b4ed-50be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=e9d24598-5abe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1486204)**

The first African-American woman in Wisconsin history to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives, Congresswoman Gwen Moore, took her official oath of office on January 4, 2005. body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39355%2E3422442%2EFVHMTu)
Derek,
I am so proud that Dave Min won in California’s 47th district, keeping the seat I currently represent blue.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241219-min?refcode=em20241219-39355&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39355_3422442_FVHMTu&akid=39355%2E3422442%2EFVHMTu)
Dave has done important work at the state level to improve the lives of Orange County families, and I know he’ll continue that work in Congress.
**But here’s the deal:** CA-47 is an incredibly competitive, purple district. We can’t take anything for granted in 2026, which is why I’m personally reaching out today.
Early money helps to lay the foundations for campaigns. The earlier a campaign receives a grassroots donation, the more effectively they can spend it.
**Dave won by 2.8% last month, so we need to do everything we can to help Dave win re-election in 2026.**
**[Will you split a $5 contribution between Dave Min’s campaign and Truth to Power today to help him prepare for a Republican opponent and millions of dollars in possible outside spending? I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t critically important.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241219-min?refcode=em20241219-39355&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39355_3422442_FVHMTu&akid=39355%2E3422442%2EFVHMTu)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241219-min?refcode=em20241219-39355&refcode2=39355_3422442_FVHMTu&akid=39355%2E3422442%2EFVHMTu)
Thank you!
Katie

**Katie Porter started Truth to Power to support Democrats who reject corporate PAC money** and are running to protect the environment, restore the nationwide right to an abortion, and create an economy that works for working people. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/truth-to-power-1?refcode=em20241219-39355&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=39355_3422442_FVHMTu&akid=39355%2E3422442%2EFVHMTu)**
---
Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. But, if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [**you can do so here**](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-ttp-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/ttp-unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=39355%2E3422442%2EFVHMTu).

To contribute via check, please address to: Truth to Power PAC, PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616

name: GallegoForArizona.com
subject: “From Sleeping on Floors to History-Making Senator” — Ruben in People Magazine
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
#

Peter - we thought you might want to read this:
[](https://people.com/ruben-gallego-upbringing-historic-career-exclusive-interview-8761899)
[**From Sleeping on Floors to History-Making Senator: How Ruben Gallego Fought Back When Childhood Took a Turn (People Magazine)**](https://people.com/ruben-gallego-upbringing-historic-career-exclusive-interview-8761899)
[READ THE ARTICLE](https://people.com/ruben-gallego-upbringing-historic-career-exclusive-interview-8761899)
It’s a journey that can only happen in America.
The son of an immigrant single mom goes from sleeping on the floor in an apartment on the south side of Chicago, to Harvard, to Iraq, to Arizona and the United States Senate.
There’s no doubt that tough fights and difficult times are ahead. But make no mistake: the American Dream is worth the struggle.
[**Check out Ruben’s feature in People here!**](https://people.com/ruben-gallego-upbringing-historic-career-exclusive-interview-8761899)
[READ THE ARTICLE](https://people.com/ruben-gallego-upbringing-historic-career-exclusive-interview-8761899)

name: Becca Balint
subject: The Musk-Trump Administration
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont**
body:
Here’s what’s happening: | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98981692/518461894/-785282667?refcode=AU_RAB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20241219_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmUxODUxNy01ZGJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GyXvQ3t2jucN42rnkMw34XWsybDArSwOUx-WH2lyt6I=)
---
Peter, Elon Musk is **_not_** the president— but he’s inserted himself into a situation that could result in a federal government shutdown. Here’s what’s happening: The House was preparing to vote on a continuing resolution (CR) with bipartisan support, which would keep the government funded until March. The government can’t run without this money. Then, Elon sent out a flurry of posts opposing the resolution and threatening to target any House Republicans who voted in favor of the CR — before Donald Trump even formed an opinion. Immediately after he sent that tweet — _or X, whatever he wants to call it now_ — dozens of House Republicans reversed their support. That’s the reality we’re living in right now. It’s absurd. And it should terrify us all. **The fact is that Elon and his billionaire buddies are not going to lose sleep if the government shuts down. Working people and our troops are the ones who’ll bear the cost — because they could have their funding delayed or be furloughed right before the holidays.** [**If Republicans can’t find their spine and stand up against Elon Musk, then we need leaders who will — now. Contribute to help me continue calling out this dysfunction and stand up against any attacks on hardworking Americans.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98981693/518461895/-1811435521?refcode=AU_RAB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20241219_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmUxODUxNy01ZGJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GyXvQ3t2jucN42rnkMw34XWsybDArSwOUx-WH2lyt6I=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98981694/518461896/-1811435521?express_lane=true&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=AU_RAB_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20241219_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmUxODUxNy01ZGJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GyXvQ3t2jucN42rnkMw34XWsybDArSwOUx-WH2lyt6I=)

name: Jared Moskowitz
subject: I’m running again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jared Moskowitz For Congress P

name: Center for American Liberty
subject: Help correct one of the worst injustices
body:
In the eyes of the cultural Marxists who control so many institutions in America today, your body doesn’t belong to you – it belongs to them. | |
---
| | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=85444b2a438d400bb81a28cc446d3ed4&_e=-QHTKWd3i1YW4MH2quSv4ZJ3lkmxxqvy91186wEg2GF0ICc1rm14CVQaPVLBsOj2UhuNjbQmAqRq2O04w6xe1foyELLHQPRKCPL5_jNzhGNUgw9uBU0tATbuK2qjF6MKn8Hhq7X0MtgZg6R8mcXOtMCEsT8jf01JL4ChUjA_A4ZizR3qCbGLFV54XBbiFCOlIbmVwP7KRhuxD06psrAKmb8hk8EVK6zFjwB-R67ckqxZ8sxUcWrJSd03yRn7Ho28YZzmu5ckKxqcu8fuh-_--7QJz-OT7RtJirZlbnYvW3098MF26CQ2W59xF_0kJl8XamPj0CgQ3DjmcCZ78GcxJ9ZyGaz9bvldlLaHk4mCzOxKObCnTd1j3yZqFte5ZRkWsHsuimOfNtQj5s5p9buaNg%3D%3D)
---
Fellow American, You have an opportunity to help correct one of the worst injustices of the Covid era. **Thou****sands of hardworking, competent people were fired –– all because they refused to get the Covid shot.** Doctors, nurses, teachers, members of the military, and many more. It’s the natural tendency of the authoritarian left to punish people for refusing to go along with its schemes. And now that we know more of the truth about Covid, the vaccine, and the shady collusion between government organizations, Big Tech and Big Pharma......they’d love nothing more than to sweep this all under the rug and pretend it never happened. **That’s why our attorneys at the Center for American Liberty initiated legal action to hold them accountable.** For starters, we filed a lawsuit against the North Carolina Symphony on behalf of three musicians fired for refusing to get the COVID vaccine._Each of them had been in the Symphony for over a decade._ **[**And if we can win this critical legal battle, we can put employers across the country on notice that they should reinstate victims of their COVID vaccine firings.**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=85444b2a438d400bb81a28cc446d3ed4&_e=-QHTKWd3i1YW4MH2quSv4ZJ3lkmxxqvy91186wEg2GF0ICc1rm14CVQaPVLBsOj2UhuNjbQmAqRq2O04w6xe1foyELLHQPRKCPL5_jNzhGNUgw9uBU0tATbuK2qjF6MKn8Hhq7X0MtgZg6R8mcXOtMCEsT8jf01JL4ChUjA_A4ZizR3qCbGLFV54XBbiFCOlIbmVwP7KRhuxD06psrAKmb8hk8EVK6zFjwB-R67ckqzwTGkRu7ZfPfINjYhPaoKSLfJhriekjsXFICbyfTs39OKWIl6hrcyAnePAqrezV3KmpJzWsFAP2J6YJ0Q9wrwDWQUppul8bAmbX2uvnsgTzwXQz3WAIq7ML-AY_2T2McSns2BJodxl8xYt4GUa69bZwTHSQrr0RtQto2QcxRel1w%3D%3D)** Plus, a generous donor has pledged to **[**match every gift**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=85444b2a438d400bb81a28cc446d3ed4&_e=-QHTKWd3i1YW4MH2quSv4ZJ3lkmxxqvy91186wEg2GF0ICc1rm14CVQaPVLBsOj2UhuNjbQmAqRq2O04w6xe1foyELLHQPRKCPL5_jNzhGNUgw9uBU0tATbuK2qjF6MKn8Hhq7X0MtgZg6R8mcXOtMCEsT8jf01JL4ChUjA_A4ZizR3qCbGLFV54XBbiFCOlIbmVwP7KRhuxD06psrAKmb8hk8EVK6zFjwB-R67ckqz1H_r_63ApPhIjMSqAshSzrim6JDWHnBbuMXvbMGup5ENwdp9cVcJWNf3s6ZT9XUgzmqUnXs92m8H6PUHQQ8MQDdgrJ_dS8ujyoBeyDeqBLrIbWvbce_1_55IJ_KvQJgsyGkB9iBOb99it_kohDW_D8TbKkwGvQJgmVUiaizTERA%3D%3D)** to the Center for American Liberty -- up to $30,000 -- between now and the end of the year! Thanks to this matching gift, your donation today will go twice as far. When the Symphony introduced their vaccine mandate, it offered both religious and medical exemptions.Dovid, Chris, and Rachel each held sincere religious beliefs, filed for exemptions, and expressed a willingness to take other measures—like regular COVID testing—to ensure safety.But the Symphony rejected ALL of them and put them on a year of UNPAID LEAVE.These three musicians were Buddhist, Christian and Jewish. **To you and me, the freedom to practice your religion is a sacred right.****So is your right to bodily autonomy.** But in the eyes of the cultural Marxists who control so many institutions in America today, your body doesn’t belong to you – it belongs to them. They’ll decide where you go... They’ll decide what you need... They’ll decide what you can have... They’ll decide what vaccines you must take...In other words: _Your individual liberties_ will cease to exist.The good news is that while cultural Marxism is popular on college campuses, legacy media press rooms, the boardrooms of woke companies and inside government bureaucracies......the vast majority of Americans are against it. This is a fight we can win – and we must win. **[**You can join me today by making a generous, tax-deductible donation to the Center for American Liberty to fund this lawsuit for medical and religious freedom, and set a precedent nationwide that puts these radicals on notice.**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=85444b2a438d400bb81a28cc446d3ed4&_e=-QHTKWd3i1YW4MH2quSv4ZJ3lkmxxqvy91186wEg2GF0ICc1rm14CVQaPVLBsOj2UhuNjbQmAqRq2O04w6xe1foyELLHQPRKCPL5_jNzhGNUgw9uBU0tATbuK2qjF6MKn8Hhq7X0MtgZg6R8mcXOtMCEsT8jf01JL4ChUjA_A4ZizR3qCbGLFV54XBbiFCOlIbmVwP7KRhuxD06psrAKmb8hk8EVK6zFjwB-R67ckqxougQvhQtfUw9j5cdVWS5iWH49SAnxIKLqXwNLdif9mcVpkd4yGR8jiSdRlA3PbGL40_sHreJcrvyLZ4QYmkHF9kBiuINLh6yLJrF1stNrsuG8G_gA8tmpp3IZw70_DkJEkZAu2ulSGk5F9Vtbc_dnEK3NmZAT7NUmx4KEjzCCGg%3D%3D)** Even after the North Carolina Symphony lifted its vaccine mandate, it refused to give these musicians their jobs back.Because it was never about “science” or “health” – it was always about control.And it’s time to tell the cultural Marxists who want to control every aspect of your life that we aren’t backing down. Remember, your $25 gift will have a $50 impact, your $100 gift becomes a $200 impact, your $500 gift becomes a $1,000 impact, and so on. I hope I can count on your support.
---
| | Sincerely,
---
Mark Trammell, Executive Director of Center for American Liberty

clean_body: In the eyes of the cultural Marxists who control so many institutions in America today, your body doesn’t belong to you – it belongs to them. | | | | Fellow American, You have an opportunity to help correct one of the worst injustices of the Covid era. Thousands of hardworking, competent people were fired –– all because they refused to get the Covid shot. Doctors, nurses, teachers, members of the military, and many more. It’s the natural tendency of the authoritarian left to punish people for refusing to go along with its schemes. And now that we know more of the truth about Covid, the vaccine, and the shady collusion between government organizations, Big Tech and Big Pharma......they’d love nothing more than to sweep this all under the rug and pretend it never happened. That’s why our attorneys at the Center for American Liberty initiated legal action to hold them accountable. For starters, we filed a lawsuit against the North Carolina Symphony on behalf of three musicians fired for refusing to get the COVID vaccine.Each of them had been in the Symphony for over a decade. Plus, a generous donor has pledged to to the Center for American Liberty -- up to $30,000 -- between now and the end of the year! Thanks to this matching gift, your donation today will go twice as far. When the Symphony introduced their vaccine mandate, it offered both religious and medical exemptions.Dovid, Chris, and Rachel each held sincere religious beliefs, filed for exemptions, and expressed a willingness to take other measures—like regular COVID testing—to ensure safety.But the Symphony rejected ALL of them and put them on a year of UNPAID LEAVE.These three musicians were Buddhist, Christian and Jewish. To you and me, the freedom to practice your religion is a sacred right.So is your right to bodily autonomy. But in the eyes of the cultural Marxists who control so many institutions in America today, your body doesn’t belong to you – it belongs to them. They’ll decide where you go... They’ll decide what you need... They’ll decide what you can have... They’ll decide what vaccines you must take...In other words: Your individual liberties will cease to exist.The good news is that while cultural Marxism is popular on college campuses, legacy media press rooms, the boardrooms of woke companies and inside government bureaucracies......the vast majority of Americans are against it. This is a fight we can win – and we must win. Even after the North Carolina Symphony lifted its vaccine mandate, it refused to give these musicians their jobs back.Because it was never about “science” or “health” – it was always about control.And it’s time to tell the cultural Marxists who want to control every aspect of your life that we aren’t backing down. Remember, your $25 gift will have a $50 impact, your $100 gift becomes a $200 impact, your $500 gift becomes a $1,000 impact, and so on. I hope I can count on your support. | | Sincerely, Mark Trammell, Executive Director of Center for American Liberty | | | | | The Center for American Liberty is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization created to fight against growing anti-free speech and anti-civil liberties trends. © The Center for American Liberty | PO Box 200942, Pittsburgh, PA 15251 |

body:

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981943/518462495/779512579?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551)
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981944/518462496/779512579?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551)
Peter,
There’s no more iconic image of California than that of the towering redwoods and beautiful coastal views. That’s [Sonoma Coast Redwoods](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981945/518462497/-651014446?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&ms=YE24_EM11_Link1&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551) – a wild place, full of life, home to some of the oldest second-growth redwoods in Sonoma County.
If you want to take a little piece of the Sonoma Coast Redwoods with you wherever you go, we have the perfect gift for you: a free sticker!
**We want to send a sticker your way in appreciation for _sticking_ up for Sonoma Coast Redwoods. [Claim your sticker now and display it proudly to help us spread the important message that redwood landscapes like these deserve to be protected!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981946/518462498/-1743229687?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&ms=YE24_EM11_Link2&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551)**
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981947/518462499/-1743229687?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&ms=YE24_EM11_Img&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551)
Save the Redwoods League is seeking to purchase and protect [Sonoma Coast Redwoods](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981948/518462500/-651014446?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&ms=YE24_EM11_Link3&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551), a stunning 1,600+ acres on the California coast. We couldn’t take on ambitious land acquisition projects like this without the help of forest lovers like YOU!
This landscape and project are symbols of an iconic California landscape and it deserves just as iconic of a sticker.
[**So, make sure to claim your free Sonoma Coast Redwoods sticker, our gift of thanks!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98981949/518462501/-1743229687?contactdata=C9mH0PASOPs4VTwkcs0t3ap8yfNp0zIUijm7D1JSINqR6NvWFpjCUiEXI2t6xJFJIvYqaU0zxa11q3KKCbQLwOUehJ80jcFYO6iA9CC/TJH2pboNudgWeQP0cpupHQcaRXTRKkbH+ibu4O3x9Vz8FQcusZ2mOECI92ShOMqqxdA=&ms=YE24_EM11_Link4&sourceid=1165968&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgyMGI3ODE3LTVlYmUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTNhZiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jzrdp4Tzmw03HY5nY4DWbHbdEGwJho6Kijv19KrWqFg=&emci=fec48738-dfbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=820b7817-5ebe-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=1504551) Whether you stick it on a laptop or a water bottle, we hope it brings a bit of redwoods beauty into your everyday life.
Thanks for all you do,
Save the Redwoods League

San Francisco, CA 94104

clean_body: An iconic property deserves an iconic sticker Peter, There’s no more iconic image of California than that of the towering redwoods and beautiful coastal views. That’s – a wild place, full of life, home to some of the oldest second-growth redwoods in Sonoma County. If you want to take a little piece of the Sonoma Coast Redwoods with you wherever you go, we have the perfect gift for you: a free sticker! We want to send a sticker your way in appreciation for sticking up for Sonoma Coast Redwoods. Save the Redwoods League is seeking to purchase and protect , a stunning 1,600+ acres on the California coast. We couldn’t take on ambitious land acquisition projects like this without the help of forest lovers like YOU! This landscape and project are symbols of an iconic California landscape and it deserves just as iconic of a sticker. Whether you stick it on a laptop or a water bottle, we hope it brings a bit of redwoods beauty into your everyday life. Thanks for all you do, Save the Redwoods League | Save the Redwoods League 111 Sutter Street, 11th Floor San Francisco, CA 94104 United States tel:415-362-2352 | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- |

subject: Trump and Elon Musk are leading us to a shutdown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sanchez
body:
Here in California, refusing to pass the spending bill will cost billions of dollars, including an estimated $2 billion for California farmers.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98982070/518462924/-1703248918?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2024-12-19_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjQ1OGIxMi01ZmJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=braDYBGvmu1z4Y2_JXn94BzVmN5KiJ2gC2X78jeXI9k=)

| Peter,

In case you haven’t been keeping up with the news this week, I’m here to fill you in:

_**Trump and House Republicans led by Elon Musk just killed a critical spending package that would have funded disaster relief, economic assistance for farmers, and so much more.**_

Now, we’re facing a potential government shutdown unless a deal is reached before midnight tomorrow. Here in California, refusing to pass the spending bill will cost billions of dollars, including an estimated $2 billion for California farmers.

This is exactly what we’ve come to expect from the Republican party. Except now, they’re letting the wealthiest man in the world, unelected Elon Musk, lead the charge to line their own pockets at the expense of American families, veterans, and seniors.

In case that wasn’t clear, **the wealthiest man in the world, who holds NO elected office and who receives government subsidies for FOUR of his companies, should not have the power to strip critical assistance for working families.**

I’ve spent my career fighting for the working class as a labor rights attorney, and in Congress, that experience has led my efforts to continue protecting our communities. Come January, I’m ready to take on the Trump-Musk administration. [_**I’ll also be working to set the groundwork for 2026 victories because we’re stronger in numbers. If you feel compelled to donate to my campaign, you can do so by clicking here or the button below:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98982076/518462929/-1667771354?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2024-12-19_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjQ1OGIxMi01ZmJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=braDYBGvmu1z4Y2_JXn94BzVmN5KiJ2gC2X78jeXI9k=)

name: Steve Daines - NRSC Chairman
subject: A new year and new opportunities
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
Help our new Senate majority lead the fight to Make America Great Again and stop the bleeding from Joe Biden’s disastrous presidency. |
Fellow American,

As 2024 comes to a close, we have a lot to be grateful for this year. President Trump will be President of the United States again, and we regained the Senate majority!

_**But now we must look to 2025.** We will still be focused on supporting Senate Republicans as they fight with President Trump to Make America Great Again. _

We can't lose this majority we worked so hard to build after just two years. **Otherwise, President Trump would be a "lame duck" president, and we would waste years of opportunities because our leaders would not have the political muscle they need.**

In 2024, many of our candidates entered their races facing 2, 3, 4, or even 5 to 1 financial deficit they were never able to close. **Unfortunately, Fellow American, this cost Republicans multiple winnable seats in the U.S. Senate.**

[**Fellow American, would you please consider making a generous year-end contribution to support our Incumbent Republican candidates and GOP nominees fighting to secure critical seats we need to HOLD our Senate majority?**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=0f3b5bbb683c49ad8e84635773a8a932&_e=RKssKsZhcZTlCSz9f9jl-yAW2GwtnyD6muMYDzrSYHUzgpdYPZdf2Pl8JRmhlc2UihCS3YY1fm5mpKyUtvvvNPrpwt0ZmSSBPaNP4dNwlUvUo9jeinSqQiidmYlNYJrbvUNLWssecZ2huO49Tj2e28zDfeG0N-4NqmXVSVdDj8XYz_hMNEfC_ctgHeruNU5Z_logBV3JxUtCtONNnmfd38ksulwfvno5I1MdymF3xHOuqiJ8MXcTrtX0IF11oCjG90761c_LkApPvhN2kJyM9Cmzh9rkmdTScnNipTPo4sELdHlf7Ex2LJ2px6Y39xBB-6XeG16Kq9fSUg6h_vc-X7DvLnYdPPvAwFtG8DDuGqWNn2dLbj7NPjXikyyHRO4N0y9jF-1EI_5HXv6pT-Ey5E3Y7suuvXRhto1PjyHGQiyVs8F_tL_hE91bi6gyyhAdm86mElBTC7td5tOscs0JIw%3D%3D)

[**CHIP IN BEFORE DECEMBER 31st ->**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=0f3b5bbb683c49ad8e84635773a8a932&_e=RKssKsZhcZTlCSz9f9jl-yAW2GwtnyD6muMYDzrSYHUzgpdYPZdf2Pl8JRmhlc2UihCS3YY1fm5mpKyUtvvvNPrpwt0ZmSSBPaNP4dNwlUvUo9jeinSqQiidmYlNYJrbvUNLWssecZ2huO49Tj2e28zDfeG0N-4NqmXVSVdDj8XYz_hMNEfC_ctgHeruNU5Z_logBV3JxUtCtONNnmfd38ksulwfvno5I1MdymF3xHOuqiJ8MXcTrtX0IF11oCjG90761c_LkApPvhN2kJyM9Cmzh9rkmdTScnNipTPo4sELdHlf7Ex2LJ2px6Y39xBBCY0QneMn7oLKYkQ-NBTZywrpm8hfV_N2c5xLfd1fF7KzHtnAukIsPwag_cPpb9JDmMU3Wupf2Rn-tmqnO0LdPwWSMFqOgeHMqqyxZljcyVu5gJYG4YHWpwgF_Y6zwYx_x7Y4c8diAYKwRPSmHEu5eQ%3D%3D)
We want our Republican incumbents and candidates to start on a solid financial footing in these can't-lose races. The Democrats have corrupt billionaires on their side and the lamestream media marketing their ideas free of charge.

We know money can be tight around the Christmas season, but we must help our candidates build war chests now so they are not stuck trying to fill a financial gap when they should be focused on winning.

clean_body: Help our new Senate majority lead the fight to Make America Great Again and stop the bleeding from Joe Biden’s disastrous presidency. | Fellow American, As 2024 comes to a close, we have a lot to be grateful for this year. President Trump will be President of the United States again, and we regained the Senate majority! But now we must look to 2025. We will still be focused on supporting Senate Republicans as they fight with President Trump to Make America Great Again. We can't lose this majority we worked so hard to build after just two years. Otherwise, President Trump would be a "lame duck" president, and we would waste years of opportunities because our leaders would not have the political muscle they need. In 2024, many of our candidates entered their races facing 2, 3, 4, or even 5 to 1 financial deficit they were never able to close. Unfortunately, Fellow American, this cost Republicans multiple winnable seats in the U.S. Senate. We want our Republican incumbents and candidates to start on a solid financial footing in these can't-lose races. The Democrats have corrupt billionaires on their side and the lamestream media marketing their ideas free of charge. We know money can be tight around the Christmas season, but we must help our candidates build war chests now so they are not stuck trying to fill a financial gap when they should be focused on winning. Last month, we were outraised by Senate Democrats - we cannot let this happen again. We want to finish December and 2024 on top. Thank you, Steve Daines Chairman, National Republican Senatorial Committee P.S. Please don't let Senate Democrats beat us again. body:

Peter,
I haven’t seen your name come through yet and wanted to make sure you caught the Governor’s email about the critical public fundraising deadline just around the corner.
_**Here’s why it matters:** Our 2024 fundraising numbers will be made public for the _first time_ shortly after our public reporting deadline on December 31._
Once our report goes live, local MAGA Republicans emboldened by Donald Trump’s return to the White House will scrutinize every detail of it, searching for any sign of vulnerability as we head into 2025.
Peter, we must hit our $200,000 end-of-year goal to leave no question that our movement is stronger than ever and ready for the fight ahead.
**[We’re getting there, but we’re counting on a big surge of support over the next two weeks to hit our mark. name: Supervisor David Canepa
subject: 2024 December e-newsletter
body:
| | | |
---
| | |
# 2024 December e-newsletter
---
|
---
| **Wishing you a happy, healthy and connected holiday season ** Dear friends, As 2024 concludes and the holidays approach, my wife Ana, son Piero and I want to wish all of you a happy, healthy and connected holiday season. Every year at this time, I love to share the family’s annual holiday card and reminisce on the days when Piero opened his first Christmas present or tasted his first cup of hot cocoa. For many, the holidays are the most wonderful time of the year, but we must remember for others it can bit lonely and isolating. The added pressures of being social, spending money, expectations from others that you should be jolly and even the sounds of Christmas music can trigger feelings of loneliness. There’s no cure for it but there are things we can all do to help ourselves and the people around us to connect in meaningful ways. In January, I sponsored a resolution declaring loneliness a public health crisis, making San Mateo County the first in the nation to do so. The resolution was prompted by U.S. Surgeon General Vivek Murthy after he declared loneliness an epidemic, noting that it can lead to poor health outcomes such as heart attack, stroke, suicide and that it impacts nearly 50% of all Americans. “Just as we need food and water, we need social connection to be healthy,” Dr. Murthy writes in the handbook “Recipes for Connection.” This holiday season, I am encouraging all of you to be sensitive to the fact that this time of the year isn’t joyful for everyone. I am challenging myself and you to be kind to strangers, to strike up a conversation with a workmate you don’t normally interact with, to call a family member you haven’t spoken with in a while, to get out of the house over the holidays and do a little volunteering and to be reasonable with what you expect of yourself and others. With that, I look forward to continuing to serve you on the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors in 2025 as we work together to improve the lives of all of our neighbors.
---
| **
I’m honored to be named Daly City’s ‘Outstanding Citizen of the Year’** I was honored to attend the 2024 Mayor’s Awards and to be named “Outstanding Citizen of the Year” by Daly City Mayor Juslyn Manalo at City Hall Monday, Dec. 16. Mayor Manalo said during the ceremony: “David Canepa has been named Outstanding Citizen of the Year for his exceptional dedication to improving the quality of life in Daly City. Through his leadership and tireless advocacy, David has secured $59,973,595 in funding for a variety of initiatives benefiting the community.” She continued: “His efforts have supported local nonprofits and churches, bolstered programs for Jefferson Union and Jefferson Elementary School Districts, and contributed to vital projects such as the Vista Grande Watershed Project and improvements to Midway Village and Gellert Park. David has also championed initiatives like Age-Friendly Cities and GotWheels!, ensuring resources and opportunities for residents of all ages. His unwavering commitment to Daly City continues to leave a lasting impact.” Throughout my 8 years serving on the Board and 8 years previous serving on the Daly City Council, I have always been committed to serving the needs of the residents of Daly City and will continue to do so in my final term. I must thank my wife Ana and son Piero for their devotion, inspiration and support as the job can be incredibly demanding. I must also thank my Chief of Staff Tony Bayudan, the best in the business, and legislative aides Mike Richardson, Bill Silverfarb, Sophia Brink, Hewan Talaktor, Bradley Murdock and Jet Penne for their outstanding commitment and the unique perspectives they bring to the county. Thank you, Daly City!
---
| **County funds new Natural History Gallery ** The San Mateo County Historical Museum in downtown Redwood City holds a very special place in my heart. The old courthouse has been a gathering place since 1858. Today, it has been transformed into an incredible museum that features interactive experiences as its exhibits are the place where you can explore the county’s rich and colorful history including its original inhabitants the Ohlone people who lived in the area 4,000 years before European explorers arrived. With the addition of the Carriage House being constructed now, the museum is taking an incredible transformation. And now I’m proud to say, with the Board of Supervisors approving my request of $500,000 in district-discretionary Measure K funds, the museum is transforming yet again with its new Natural History Gallery. The gallery will provide insights into the geological, biological and paleontological history of the Peninsula. Visitors will have the opportunity to explore a collection of rare fossils and skeletal remains, shedding light on the prehistoric evolution of the region, including mammoth mammal that inhabited the area 12,000 years ago during the Ice Age. As a former Board of Director to the Historical Association, I want to thank the current Board and the President’s Advisory Board and especially the museum’s president Mitch Postel for making this new gallery a reality, which is scheduled to open in 2026.
---
| **Turkey giveaway helps combat the holiday blues ** ** ** I was proud to partner with Friends of Old Town in a longstanding annual tradition to give free turkeys to people who need them most at the Morelos Hall in South San Francisco Saturday, Nov. 23. Old Town is one of South San Francisco’s oldest and most vibrant neighborhoods and the people who live there are some of the most welcoming and gracious. But many in this working-class community are older adults, some isolated and lonely, and others who are food insecure. This longstanding free turkey giveaway is an expression of gratitude and a way to help people connect at a time when some people might be feeling depressed and have the holiday blues. Special thanks to Sociedad Mutualista Mexicana Morelo, Painters and Drywall Finishers Local 913, Carpet, Linoleum and Soft Tile Workers Local 12 and South San Francisco Scavenger for sponsoring and volunteering at this event.
---
| **San Bruno Mountain Park to get new Interpretive Center, picnic area** I’m proud to announce San Bruno Mountain Park will get a new Interpretive Center and picnic area in the summer of 2025, a project I have partnered with the County Parks Department to fund. The San Bruno Mountain Day Use Improvement project is a capital improvement project that focuses on the replacement and modernization of an antiquated restroom building; the addition of an open-air interpretive pavilion; re-arrangement of the picnic sites and replacement of aged tables and BBQ grills; and improvements to path of travel, ADA accessibility, and landscaping. The new building will include 4 all-gender and ADA accessible toilet rooms with sinks and changing tables. There will be a 640 sq.-ft. covered area for use as an interpretive pavilion that will feature interpretive panels and multimedia, exhibit space, and/or gathering areas for interpretive events and public use. The current picnic area includes 14 dispersed picnic sites, 21 tables, seven grills, seven trash and recycle bins, and two water fountains. The updated picnic area will reduce the total number of sites to nine sites with 23 tables, with each site having 1, 2, or 4 table groupings. Six of the nine sites will be fully accessible to people of all abilities. Path of travel improvements will also be made connecting each of the ADA accessible picnic site through a circular paved pathway. Other path of travel improvements includes walkways around the new restroom building, re-positioning the existing ADA parking stalls, and creating a new connecting path between the parking lot and day use area. The updates to the picnic sites and general landscape for the day use area also include addition of timber benches, tree management and native vegetation plantings.
---
| **County pilots basic income benefit for survivors of domestic violence ** The Board of Supervisors established a new year-long pilot program in December to help survivors of domestic violence with at least one minor child ease the financial burden of leaving their abuser, a program I sponsored with Supervisor Noelia Corzo. The Board approved $348,000 for the Guaranteed Income Program for Domestic Violence Survivors: $240,000 for dedicated monthly payments of $1,000 to 20 eligible individuals and the remainder to administer and evaluate it. Community Overcoming Relationship Abuse, CORA, will operate the program through June 30, 2026, and is also contributing $10,000 in private funding toward the payments. Money should not be the sticking point of whether someone should stay or go. No person should feel trapped in an abusive relationship because they don’t have the economic ability to walk away. If these funds help determine someone’s decision to protect themselves and their children without fear of not having the means to live, it is money well spent. The funds come with no spending restrictions, allowing recipients to use the stipend as they wish for needs like transportation, food and shelter. The first payments are expected to begin in January 2025. Supervisor Corzo and I recommended the unconditional, unrestricted monthly payments to help those leaving domestic violence situations navigate common obstacles such as childcare, new housing and legal costs. Without financial independence and the ability to cover expenses, some may remain in abusive relationships because they are financially dependent.
---
| **County emphasizes a mental health-focused lens across all programs ** I am excited to announce the Board of Supervisors passed a resolution I sponsored that emphasizes a mental health-focused lens across all of San Mateo County’s goals, programs, and policies. The resolution reflects our commitment to the well-being of our community, aiming to make mental health a guiding principle in everything we do. Our Measure K priorities — affordable housing, support for children and families, and emergency preparedness — are already strengthening our community. These areas are crucial to mental wellness, providing stability, safety, and a sense of belonging. But we must go further. With this mental health-focused lens, we can create an environment where mental wellness considerations are embedded into policies and programs at every level, promoting resilience and reducing stigma. This resolution calls on us to adopt a multifaceted approach that includes increasing access to mental health services, promoting social connection, and supporting vulnerable populations facing isolation, homelessness, or other challenges. It also emphasizes the need to address mental health disparities affecting vulnerable communities, low-income individuals, and people with disabilities, ensuring that everyone has equal access to resources and support. With this mental health-focused approach, we envision a San Mateo County where all policies and programs — from housing and transportation to public spaces — foster connection and mental wellness. Together, we can create a San Mateo County where everyone has the opportunity to thrive—mentally, physically, and socially.
---
| **Interns wanted to join our team ** We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=14164bb122&e=c0babbc643). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org ](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org )
---
| **Battling food insecurity **
Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: **CalFresh:**
Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: [https://www.cdss.ca.gov/food-nutrition/calfresh](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=b64b1af07c&e=c0babbc643) Contact: (650) 802-6576, [antorres@smcgov.org](mailto:antorres@smcgov.org) **Daly City Partnership:**
DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: [https://www.dcpartnership.org/covid19/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=e905a73038&e=c0babbc643) **Second Harvest Food Bank:**
Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: [https://www.shfb.org/get-food/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=8657061b12&e=c0babbc643) Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 In friendship,
David J. Canepa
---
|
---
|

|

clean_body: | | | | | | | 2024 December e-newsletter | | Wishing you a happy, healthy and connected holiday season Dear friends, As 2024 concludes and the holidays approach, my wife Ana, son Piero and I want to wish all of you a happy, healthy and connected holiday season. Every year at this time, I love to share the family’s annual holiday card and reminisce on the days when Piero opened his first Christmas present or tasted his first cup of hot cocoa. For many, the holidays are the most wonderful time of the year, but we must remember for others it can bit lonely and isolating. The added pressures of being social, spending money, expectations from others that you should be jolly and even the sounds of Christmas music can trigger feelings of loneliness. There’s no cure for it but there are things we can all do to help ourselves and the people around us to connect in meaningful ways. In January, I sponsored a resolution declaring loneliness a public health crisis, making San Mateo County the first in the nation to do so. The resolution was prompted by U.S. Surgeon General Vivek Murthy after he declared loneliness an epidemic, noting that it can lead to poor health outcomes such as heart attack, stroke, suicide and that it impacts nearly 50% of all Americans. “Just as we need food and water, we need social connection to be healthy,” Dr. Murthy writes in the handbook “Recipes for Connection.” This holiday season, I am encouraging all of you to be sensitive to the fact that this time of the year isn’t joyful for everyone. I am challenging myself and you to be kind to strangers, to strike up a conversation with a workmate you don’t normally interact with, to call a family member you haven’t spoken with in a while, to get out of the house over the holidays and do a little volunteering and to be reasonable with what you expect of yourself and others. With that, I look forward to continuing to serve you on the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors in 2025 as we work together to improve the lives of all of our neighbors. | I’m honored to be named Daly City’s ‘Outstanding Citizen of the Year’ I was honored to attend the 2024 Mayor’s Awards and to be named “Outstanding Citizen of the Year” by Daly City Mayor Juslyn Manalo at City Hall Monday, Dec. 16. Mayor Manalo said during the ceremony: “David Canepa has been named Outstanding Citizen of the Year for his exceptional dedication to improving the quality of life in Daly City. Through his leadership and tireless advocacy, David has secured $59,973,595 in funding for a variety of initiatives benefiting the community.” She continued: “His efforts have supported local nonprofits and churches, bolstered programs for Jefferson Union and Jefferson Elementary School Districts, and contributed to vital projects such as the Vista Grande Watershed Project and improvements to Midway Village and Gellert Park. David has also championed initiatives like Age-Friendly Cities and GotWheels!, ensuring resources and opportunities for residents of all ages. His unwavering commitment to Daly City continues to leave a lasting impact.” Throughout my 8 years serving on the Board and 8 years previous serving on the Daly City Council, I have always been committed to serving the needs of the residents of Daly City and will continue to do so in my final term. I must thank my wife Ana and son Piero for their devotion, inspiration and support as the job can be incredibly demanding. I must also thank my Chief of Staff Tony Bayudan, the best in the business, and legislative aides Mike Richardson, Bill Silverfarb, Sophia Brink, Hewan Talaktor, Bradley Murdock and Jet Penne for their outstanding commitment and the unique perspectives they bring to the county. Thank you, Daly City! | County funds new Natural History Gallery The San Mateo County Historical Museum in downtown Redwood City holds a very special place in my heart. The old courthouse has been a gathering place since 1858. Today, it has been transformed into an incredible museum that features interactive experiences as its exhibits are the place where you can explore the county’s rich and colorful history including its original inhabitants the Ohlone people who lived in the area 4,000 years before European explorers arrived. With the addition of the Carriage House being constructed now, the museum is taking an incredible transformation. And now I’m proud to say, with the Board of Supervisors approving my request of $500,000 in district-discretionary Measure K funds, the museum is transforming yet again with its new Natural History Gallery. The gallery will provide insights into the geological, biological and paleontological history of the Peninsula. Visitors will have the opportunity to explore a collection of rare fossils and skeletal remains, shedding light on the prehistoric evolution of the region, including mammoth mammal that inhabited the area 12,000 years ago during the Ice Age. As a former Board of Director to the Historical Association, I want to thank the current Board and the President’s Advisory Board and especially the museum’s president Mitch Postel for making this new gallery a reality, which is scheduled to open in 2026. | Turkey giveaway helps combat the holiday blues I was proud to partner with Friends of Old Town in a longstanding annual tradition to give free turkeys to people who need them most at the Morelos Hall in South San Francisco Saturday, Nov. 23. Old Town is one of South San Francisco’s oldest and most vibrant neighborhoods and the people who live there are some of the most welcoming and gracious. But many in this working-class community are older adults, some isolated and lonely, and others who are food insecure. This longstanding free turkey giveaway is an expression of gratitude and a way to help people connect at a time when some people might be feeling depressed and have the holiday blues. Special thanks to Sociedad Mutualista Mexicana Morelo, Painters and Drywall Finishers Local 913, Carpet, Linoleum and Soft Tile Workers Local 12 and South San Francisco Scavenger for sponsoring and volunteering at this event. | San Bruno Mountain Park to get new Interpretive Center, picnic area I’m proud to announce San Bruno Mountain Park will get a new Interpretive Center and picnic area in the summer of 2025, a project I have partnered with the County Parks Department to fund. The San Bruno Mountain Day Use Improvement project is a capital improvement project that focuses on the replacement and modernization of an antiquated restroom building; the addition of an open-air interpretive pavilion; re-arrangement of the picnic sites and replacement of aged tables and BBQ grills; and improvements to path of travel, ADA accessibility, and landscaping. The new building will include 4 all-gender and ADA accessible toilet rooms with sinks and changing tables. There will be a 640 sq.-ft. covered area for use as an interpretive pavilion that will feature interpretive panels and multimedia, exhibit space, and/or gathering areas for interpretive events and public use. The current picnic area includes 14 dispersed picnic sites, 21 tables, seven grills, seven trash and recycle bins, and two water fountains. The updated picnic area will reduce the total number of sites to nine sites with 23 tables, with each site having 1, 2, or 4 table groupings. Six of the nine sites will be fully accessible to people of all abilities. Path of travel improvements will also be made connecting each of the ADA accessible picnic site through a circular paved pathway. Other path of travel improvements includes walkways around the new restroom building, re-positioning the existing ADA parking stalls, and creating a new connecting path between the parking lot and day use area. The updates to the picnic sites and general landscape for the day use area also include addition of timber benches, tree management and native vegetation plantings. | County pilots basic income benefit for survivors of domestic violence The Board of Supervisors established a new year-long pilot program in December to help survivors of domestic violence with at least one minor child ease the financial burden of leaving their abuser, a program I sponsored with Supervisor Noelia Corzo. The Board approved $348,000 for the Guaranteed Income Program for Domestic Violence Survivors: $240,000 for dedicated monthly payments of $1,000 to 20 eligible individuals and the remainder to administer and evaluate it. Community Overcoming Relationship Abuse, CORA, will operate the program through June 30, 2026, and is also contributing $10,000 in private funding toward the payments. Money should not be the sticking point of whether someone should stay or go. No person should feel trapped in an abusive relationship because they don’t have the economic ability to walk away. If these funds help determine someone’s decision to protect themselves and their children without fear of not having the means to live, it is money well spent. The funds come with no spending restrictions, allowing recipients to use the stipend as they wish for needs like transportation, food and shelter. The first payments are expected to begin in January 2025. Supervisor Corzo and I recommended the unconditional, unrestricted monthly payments to help those leaving domestic violence situations navigate common obstacles such as childcare, new housing and legal costs. Without financial independence and the ability to cover expenses, some may remain in abusive relationships because they are financially dependent. | County emphasizes a mental health-focused lens across all programs I am excited to announce the Board of Supervisors passed a resolution I sponsored that emphasizes a mental health-focused lens across all of San Mateo County’s goals, programs, and policies. The resolution reflects our commitment to the well-being of our community, aiming to make mental health a guiding principle in everything we do. These areas are crucial to mental wellness, providing stability, safety, and a sense of belonging. But we must go further. This resolution calls on us to adopt a multifaceted approach that includes increasing access to mental health services, promoting social connection, and supporting vulnerable populations facing isolation, homelessness, or other challenges. It also emphasizes the need to address mental health disparities affecting vulnerable communities, low-income individuals, and people with disabilities, ensuring that everyone has equal access to resources and support. With this mental health-focused approach, we envision a San Mateo County where all policies and programs — from housing and transportation to public spaces — foster connection and mental wellness. Hey Peter, it’s Ritchie.

I recently sent over a poll on the No Kings Act to gauge where voters (like you!) stand — **and I’m still waiting on your response.**

We have limited time to act on the No Kings Act and stop Presidents from becoming above the law — but we need massive public support: [**If you could take 1 minute to complete this poll, I would really appreciate it! »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98982425/518463904/1438825910?refcode=RTFC_20241219_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWQ2OWQyOS02MWJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KFhhU63aymilnKYSo3wOfyYpCdbpCmsFNrgu5zGRTTw=&emci=f430ea34-34be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=49d69d29-61be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=34594689) | [CLICK HERE TO TAKE THE NO KINGS ACT POLL »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98982426/518463905/1438825910?refcode=RTFC_20241219_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWQ2OWQyOS02MWJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KFhhU63aymilnKYSo3wOfyYpCdbpCmsFNrgu5zGRTTw=&emci=f430ea34-34be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=49d69d29-61be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=34594689)

Thank you so much,
_Ritchie_

(You can read my original message below.)
**Peter, in July, the Supreme Court ruled in _Trump vs. the United States_ that Presidents and Vice Presidents, from here on out, will have unlimited authority and immunity from prosecution.** _I don’t have to tell you how dangerous this could be, Peter!_

**In September, Senator Chuck Schumer introduced the _No Kings Act_ in response** to ensure that no President is above the law, because no one — much less the President of the United States — should be free from accountability or consequences.

**But in November, Trump won the presidency...** and with it, immunity and unchecked authority.

Now, we have limited time to act on the No Kings Act and stop Presidents from becoming above the law — but we need massive public support:
[**That’s why I need to know, Peter: Do you support the No Kings Act?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98982427/518463906/1438825910?refcode=RTFC_20241219_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWQ2OWQyOS02MWJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KFhhU63aymilnKYSo3wOfyYpCdbpCmsFNrgu5zGRTTw=&emci=f430ea34-34be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=49d69d29-61be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=34594689)
[YES, I DO: NO ONE IS ABOVE THE LAW! »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98982428/518463907/1438825910?refcode=RTFC_20241219_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWQ2OWQyOS02MWJlLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KFhhU63aymilnKYSo3wOfyYpCdbpCmsFNrgu5zGRTTw=&emci=f430ea34-34be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=49d69d29-61be-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=34594689)

www.torres.nyc

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

clean_body: | = Hey Peter, it’s Ritchie. I recently sent over a poll on the No Kings Act to gauge where voters (like you!) stand — and I’m still waiting on your response. We have limited time to act on the No Kings Act and stop Presidents from becoming above the law — but we need massive public support: | Thank you so much, Ritchie (You can read my original message below.) Peter, in July, the Supreme Court ruled in Trump vs. the United States that Presidents and Vice Presidents, from here on out, will have unlimited authority and immunity from prosecution. I don’t have to tell you how dangerous this could be, Peter! In September, Senator Chuck Schumer introduced the No Kings Act in response to ensure that no President is above the law, because no one — much less the President of the United States — should be free from accountability or consequences. But in November, Trump won the presidency... and with it, immunity and unchecked authority. Now, we have limited time to act on the No Kings Act and stop Presidents from becoming above the law — but we need massive public support: Thank you for your response, — Ritchie | | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458 www.torres.nyc Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in. Peter, please, consider making a =. To make the most powerful lasting impact and to join Ritchie in the fight for progressive values in Congress, make a=. Click here to . Click here to . | | = =

subject: Here’s the situation...
body:

Derek — We're about to ask you to [**donate directly to the DNC.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2024?amount=3&refcode=em_20241219_2429_fr&refcode2=2429_22930333_XmvkTP&akid=2429%2E22930333%2EXmvkTP) If you give us a moment of your time, we’ll explain why your support is so important right now. Here’s the situation: When this month ends, we’ll be legally required to report how much we’ve raised. As they prepare to roll out their Project 2025 agenda, Trump and his allies would love nothing more than to see that Democrats are exhausted and ready to throw in the towel. They’re counting on all of us giving up. We can't let that happen. That’s why reaching our goals is so important. We need to show everyone that **Democrats are not done fighting.** We hope this message has shown you the urgency and importance of your contribution. |
Dear Friends and Family,
As we come together to celebrate this wonderful season, I want to take a moment to wish you all a Merry Christmas, Happy Hanukkah and a Joyous New Year. This time of year reminds us of the blessings we share, and I am truly grateful for each of you.
In the spirit of giving, I want to highlight two remarkable organizations that are making a significant impact in our communities. [_**The Organization for Social Media Safety **_](https://drasifmahmood.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=306598b2f3a0f328de4c6676e&id=0f2b867553&e=7b1b739a89)is dedicated to combating the widespread bigotry and hate that permeate our online spaces. Their critical work protects individuals from dangers like cyberbullying and mental health crises.
Equally important is [**Hope the Mission**](https://drasifmahmood.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=306598b2f3a0f328de4c6676e&id=380522fc88&e=7b1b739a89) , an organization striving to eliminate poverty, hunger, and homelessness. Their commitment to providing immediate assistance and long-term solutions upholds the dignity of every individual in need. Their impactful work has transformed thousands of lives by providing shelter, meals, and essential services to those facing adversity in our community.
As a member of the Board of Directors for both organizations, I have witnessed firsthand the transformative impact of their work. This holiday season, as we reflect on our blessings, I urge you to consider making a donation to support these essential causes. Your generosity has the power to create lasting change and uplift our most vulnerable neighbors, helping to build stronger, more resilient communities. For more information, please click on the links above, and to make a meaningful contribution, use the buttons below. Together, we can make a profound difference.
Thank you for being part of my life. Wishing you all love, joy, and peace during this festive season and throughout the coming year.
Sincerely,
Asif

---
